title: Phase 4 Research: CLI Design
description: Research the CLI architecture. Cover: thin Go binary vs Maggie script vs single binary with subcommands, IPC protocol for CLI-to-daemon communication, command structure (spawn/dismiss/list/bbs/mail/workflow/telemetry etc), agent-facing commands (what agents type in tmux), argument parsing, output formatting. Should the CLI be a Go cobra app like current cub, or something else? Reference imp-castle's internal/cli/ (39 commands). Output: docs/research/phase-4-cli.md
close_reason: Research complete: docs/research/phase-4-cli.md covers CLI architecture, IPC protocol, command structure, output formatting, and comparison with imp-castle

title: Cross-phase contradiction analysis of research documents
description:
Review all 10 research documents in docs/research/ (phase-0 through phase-9) and identify any contradictions, inconsistencies, or conflicting assumptions between phases. For each contradiction found, document:
1. Which phases conflict
2. The specific contradiction (quote both sides)
3. A recommended resolution

Areas to watch for:
- IPC protocol choices (JSON-RPC vs gRPC Connect vs other) - do all phases agree?
- Storage layer assumptions (SQLite vs BadgerDB vs image persistence) - who stores what?
- Agent model (Maggie objects vs Go structs) - consistent across phases?
- CLI architecture (Go binary vs Maggie script) - one answer?
- Config format and paths - consistent naming?
- Tuplespace schema and categories - same vocabulary everywhere?
- Mail-as-tuple vs separate mail system
- Daemon responsibilities - any overlap/gaps between phases?
- Go interop primitives - does phase 0 cover everything later phases need?
- Workflow engine - CUE loading assumptions consistent with phase 0 interop?

Output: docs/research/cross-phase-contradiction-analysis.md
close_reason: Analysis complete: 12 contradictions identified across 10 research phases (4 hard, 5 soft, 3 minor)

title: Phase 3 Research: Agent Lifecycle & Identity
description: Research agent lifecycle management in Maggie. Cover: Agent as first-class Maggie object (identity, role, state, mailbox), spawn/dismiss/respawn flows, tmux session creation and management via Go primitives, git worktree creation/cleanup, agent registry persistence, name generation, environment variable injection, agent liveness detection, crash recovery with state preservation. Reference imp-castle's internal/agent/, internal/tmux/, internal/git/, internal/state/, internal/impname/. Output: docs/research/phase-3-agent-lifecycle.md

title: Phase 6 Research: Workflow Engine
description: Research the workflow engine design. Cover: CUE workflow definition loading and validation, state machine executor in Maggie, step types (spawn/wait/evaluate/dismiss/gate), aspects (cross-cutting step injection), workflow instance tracking, approval gates, timeout handling. Then explore what a Smalltalk-native workflow DSL might look like as a future alternative to CUE. Reference imp-castle's internal/workflow/. Output: docs/research/phase-6-workflows.md

title: Resolve cross-phase contradictions in research documents
description:
Update all research documents in docs/research/ to resolve the 12 contradictions identified in cross-phase-contradiction-analysis.md. Apply these resolutions:

HARD CONTRADICTIONS:
C1: Phase 6 - Replace all BadgerDB references with SQLite. Workflow state lives in SQLite, not Badger. Update executor struct, persistence descriptions, bucket/key patterns to SQL tables.
C2: Phase 6 - Redesign Wait and Gate steps to use tuplespace instead of mail. Wait polls for event tuples (task_done), Gate writes gate_request tuples and polls for gate_response tuples.
C3: Phase 3 - Clarify that agent lifecycle operations go through daemon IPC. The Go code in Phase 3 becomes daemon-side implementation behind the IPC interface. CLI never calls tmux/git directly.
C4: Phase 0 - Remove CUE from gowrap candidates. Add a section for hand-written CUE primitives explaining the deep integration needed (module loading, two-phase compilation, unification, context injection).

SOFT INCONSISTENCIES:
I1: Phase 0 - Standardize on modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go). Update dependency list and gowrap config.
I2: Phases 2, 4, 9 - Standardize all env vars on PP_ prefix.
I3: Phases 0, 4, 9 - Standardize binary name as pp. Fix Phase 0 Makefile output and Phase 9 command examples.
I4: Phase 3 - Change tmux session prefix from cub- to pp-.
I5: Phase 7 - Scope the 'no persistent DuckDB state' claim to analytics only, note Phase 8 uses DuckDB as in-memory buffer.

MINOR GAPS:
G1: Phase 1 - Fix convention promotion scan to use correct category.
G2: Phase 3 - Add Maggie adaptation section following patterns from Phases 1 and 2 (agent state in tuplespace, Go for system ops via primitives, Maggie for orchestration).
G3: Phase 6 - Note that _ctx is internal to workflow engine, step results also written to tuplespace for observability.

Be surgical - only change what's needed to resolve contradictions. Don't rewrite entire documents.
close_reason: All 12 contradictions resolved in research docs

title: Phase 0 Research: Project Setup & Go Interop Primitives
description: Research how to set up the procyon-park project structure, build system (single binary packaging), and critically: the Go interop primitives Maggie needs. Cover: SQLite bindings, DuckDB bindings, process exec (for git/tmux), Unix socket server/client, file I/O, TOML parsing, CUE evaluation. Survey Maggie's current primitive system and design the interop layer. Output: docs/research/phase-0-project-setup.md
close_reason: Research complete: docs/research/phase-0-project-setup.md covers project structure, build system, and all 7 Go interop primitives

title: Phase 9 Research: Config, Repo Registry & Work Tracking
description: Research configuration and supporting systems. Cover: TOML config (global + per-repo), feature flags with precedence (env > repo > global > default), repository registry (repos.json equivalent), work tracker interface and beads integration, onboarding/setup validation, hub system for future multi-node. Reference imp-castle's internal/config/, internal/repo/, internal/worktracker/. Output: docs/research/phase-9-config-and-registry.md

title: Phase 1 Research: Tuplespace (BBS) in Maggie
description: Research reimplementing the Linda-style tuplespace in Maggie. Cover: Tuple representation as Maggie objects, the 4 Linda ops (out/in/rd/scan), pattern matching design, SQLite persistence via Go interop, blocking semantics using Maggie channels/processes, tuple categories (fact/convention/task/claim/obstacle/need/artifact/event/notification), lifecycle classes (furniture/session/ephemeral), TTL and GC, mail-as-tuple concept. Reference imp-castle's internal/bbs/ package. Output: docs/research/phase-1-tuplespace.md

title: Phase 5 Research: Priming & Agent Instructions
description: Research the agent priming system. Cover: role-based instruction templates (king/cub/reviewer/merge-handler), how to store and render templates in Maggie, BBS-aware instruction transforms (replacing mail references with tuplespace commands), custom instruction overrides, how agents learn the CLI and tuplespace vocabulary. Reference imp-castle's internal/prime/ and templates/. Output: docs/research/phase-5-priming.md

title: Phase 7 Research: Analytics & Storage Tiers
description: Research the analytics and tiered storage system. Cover: DuckDB warm tier for archived tuples, Parquet export from SQLite, GC policies (TTL expiry, stale claim detection, convention promotion, escalation detection), analytics queries (agent performance, obstacle clustering, convention effectiveness), cold tier archival to S3, synthesis (LLM-powered knowledge extraction). Reference imp-castle's internal/bbs/gc.go, analytics.go, synthesis.go. Output: docs/research/phase-7-analytics.md
close_reason: Research complete: docs/research/phase-7-analytics.md covers all requested topics

title: Procyon Park: Cleanroom Cub-Castle Reimplementation Research
description: Epic covering all research phases for reimplementing imp-castle in Maggie (Smalltalk-in-Go). Each phase produces a research document in docs/research/ covering architecture, design decisions, Maggie idioms, and implementation plan.

title: Phase 2 Research: Daemon Architecture
description: Research the Maggie daemon design. Cover: long-running VM image as daemon, IPC server (Unix socket JSON-RPC vs gRPC Connect), image checkpointing and crash recovery strategy, polling loops vs event-driven design in Maggie, startup sequence, PID file management, graceful shutdown. How does the Maggie eval service map to daemon IPC? Reference imp-castle's internal/daemon/ package. Output: docs/research/phase-2-daemon.md

title: Phase 8 Research: Telemetry & OTEL Integration
description: Research telemetry system design. Cover: SDLC event categories (agent/task/mail/git/session/error/workflow), event storage in BadgerDB vs SQLite, OTEL receiver (gRPC trace/metric/log ingestion), query engine for historical analysis, Parquet export, how Maggie processes can emit telemetry natively. Reference imp-castle's internal/telemetry/ and internal/otel/. Output: docs/research/phase-8-telemetry.md
